description: In 2013, Michael Dell and Silver Lake Partners launched a $24.9 billion bid to take Dell Inc. private to navigate a pivot away from declining PC markets. The move faced intense opposition from activist investor Carl Icahn, creating a high-stakes shareholder battle that tested investor sentiment on the company's long-term viability.

key_events.description: Announcement of the $24.9 billion deal offer.
Carl Icahn launches public campaign against the valuation.
Special Committee issues rebuttal to activist claims.
Shareholders approve the buyout by a slim margin.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring the impact of activist investor disruption on corporate strategy and shareholder sentiment.
